intro: Pleurospermum delavayi, also known as Delavay's carrot or Delavay's angelica, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of China. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of Pleurospermum delavayi include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or a tincture, and it is also used as a component in various herbal formulations, such as cough syrups and expectorants.
